text:
Visual Question Answering (VQA) involves interpreting complex visual scenes to provide accurate natural language responses to specific queries. Despite significant advancements in computer vision and natural language processing, VQA systems often struggle with suboptimal feature representation, specifically the inability of image features to capture fine-grained scene details and the failure of text models to fully encode linguistic nuances. To address these limitations, this paper proposes a dual-pronged approach utilizing multiscale feature extraction and fusion for both modalities. 

On the visual side, the framework leverages a pre-trained deep residual network to extract features from various network layers, employing a top-down fusion strategy to integrate low-level spatial details with high-level semantic information. On the textual side, a multiscale methodology is introduced to simultaneously capture word-level, phrase-level, and sentence-level characteristics, ensuring a more holistic representation of the question. Experimental results demonstrate that integrating these multiscale features significantly enhances the performance of VQA models. Furthermore, the study identifies optimal feature combination schemes that improve accuracy while maintaining computational efficiency within bilinear fusion frameworks.
